tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine and chloride, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but negative for nitrites, leukocytes, and blood. HbA1c is low, with normal glucose. Findings suggest possible renal impairment and proteinuria, with no clear evidence of urinary tract infection. Further evaluation of renal function and possible causes of proteinuria is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
